description_long: This is a large, heavy-lift helicopter with a distinctive skeletal fuselage and a bright orange and black color scheme. The helicopter features a long, narrow body with a spacious central section, likely designed for cargo or specialized equipment transport. It has six long, black-and-white striped main rotor blades and a tail boom that extends far behind the main body, ending in a vertical stabilizer with a tail rotor. The cockpit is located at the front, with multiple large, angular windows providing wide visibility for the pilots. The landing gear consists of two robust, downward-curving skids attached to the fuselage by sturdy supports. The overall design suggests a utilitarian aircraft built for heavy-duty operations, such as firefighting, construction, or urban rescue missions.
description: Large orange and black heavy-lift helicopter with six main rotor blades, a long tail boom, and robust landing skids. Features a spacious central body and a multi-windowed cockpit.
description_view_0: Side view showing the elongated fuselage, six main rotor blades, and the full length of the tail boom with the tail rotor visible.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the wide cockpit windows, the symmetrical arrangement of the landing skids, and the central rotor hub.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the cargo section, rotor blades, and the tail structure with the tail rotor in profile.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the tail boom, tail rotor, and the alignment of the landing skids beneath the fuselage.
attribute_rotor_blades: Six long, straight main rotor blades, each with alternating black and white stripes and yellow tips, likely made of composite material; blades are evenly spaced and extend far beyond the fuselage.
attribute_tail_rotor: Tail rotor is present on the left side of the tail boom, featuring four short blades with a green and black color scheme; blades are oriented vertically and appear to be unshrouded.
attribute_landing_skids: Two robust, downward-curving metal skids painted orange with black supports, connected by crossbars for stability; designed for heavy loads and rough terrain.
attribute_cockpit_windows: Multiple large, angular cockpit windows wrap around the front and sides, framed in black; windows are clear with a slight tint, providing wide visibility.
